scientific_question: Does the edge-only projected inverse-shortest-path baseline stop improving because the 20-epoch optimization budget is too short, or because its representation has reached a stable error floor?

development.sampling: all eligible records after excluding every index in the previous validation manifest

confirmation_a.allowed_use: one-shot comparison after the configuration is frozen

confirmation_b.allowed_use: one-shot comparison after the configuration is frozen

data_policy.disjointness: development, confirmation A, confirmation B, and the previously used fixed validation sample must have disjoint source indices
data_policy.test_policy: Do not open, deserialize, query, or report any test split in this study.

model.solver: batch projected subgradient on edge multipliers
model.objective: mean inverse-shortest-path regret plus lambda/(2m) times squared distance from q=1

convergence_grid.execution: 10-percent trajectories may run concurrently; full-data trajectories run sequentially to bound memory

controls.name: q_equals_1
controls.purpose: unlearned baseline under the new development block

controls.name: full_eta1e-4_t20
controls.purpose: reproduce the prior optimization budget and verify it is an exact prefix of the longer deterministic trajectory

predeclared_decision_rules.successful_run: process completes within 900 seconds, all reported losses are finite, and fewer than 1 percent of edge multipliers are at either projection boundary
predeclared_decision_rules.primary_configuration: among successful full-data trajectories choose the lowest development aggregate relative regret; if losses differ by at most 1e-5 choose higher edge F1, then smaller eta0
predeclared_decision_rules.convergence_evidence: strong if early stopping fires after four validation events without a cumulative 1e-5 improvement; otherwise report the last-20-epoch improvement and explicitly retain an optimization-budget caveat
predeclared_decision_rules.capacity_followup: introduce a minimal global turn-feature model only if first-divergence errors remain concentrated at complex junctions or systematic turn classes after controlling for route length and unseen edges
predeclared_decision_rules.confirmation_rule: evaluate the three frozen models once on both confirmation blocks; never use either block to choose another eta, epoch, lambda, feature, or checkpoint

bounded_runtime_policy.extension_beyond_100_epochs: not automatic; requires a new declared protocol
